title: Vulnerability candidate (LLM output)
description: Structured output the Claude Code vuln-finder skill emits per candidate. The symex-validator MCP consumes this shape directly.

source_file.description: Absolute path to the C/C++ source file.

function_name.description: Function containing the suspected sink; the symex harness will target this function.

sink_line.description: Line of the suspected dangerous operation (reporting only).

rationale.description: Short explanation of why the LLM believes this is a bug. One to three sentences.

size_bytes.description: For pointers: size of the backing buffer. 0 means 'pass NULL' (useful for CWE-476).

preconditions.description: Informal constraints the caller would need to satisfy (e.g. 'len > 16'). Not fed to KLEE — KLEE discovers them — but useful for the report.

assumptions.description: Opt-in LLM bounds pasted verbatim into klee_assume(). Reference only tainted_inputs by name. Example: ['len <= 64', 'mode < 4']. Ignored unless use_bounds=true on the verify call.

loop_bounds.description: Opt-in per-variable caps. {var: N} -> klee_assume(var <= N). Use when the LLM can bound a loop counter or a size field semantically (e.g. 'len is a packet length <= 1500'). Ignored unless use_bounds=true.

use_bounds.description: If true, assumptions and loop_bounds are applied on the first verify run. Recommended workflow: start with use_bounds=true for a fast tight run, and set auto_relax_on_infeasible=true so the server retries without bounds if needed.

auto_relax_on_infeasible.description: If true and the bounded run is infeasible, server automatically retries once with use_bounds=false and returns the relaxed verdict.

reachability.description: Optional Phase-2 reachability query run after the bug is confirmed in the function. LLM-picked entry_function (a nearby exported API, not main) and target_function (usually the same as function_name).
